sessions.title: Building Production RAG Systems with Foundry IQ
sessions.speaker: Dr. Maria Santos
sessions.description: Deep dive into building production-grade RAG systems using Azure AI Search and Foundry IQ. Covers knowledge base creation, agentic retrieval, query planning, and evaluation strategies.

sessions.title: Agentic Retrieval: Beyond Simple RAG
sessions.speaker: James Chen
sessions.description: How agents use query planning, multi-hop retrieval, and cross-source reasoning to go beyond traditional top-k search. Live demo of Foundry IQ agentic retrieval pipeline.

sessions.title: Context Engineering for Reliable Agents
sessions.speaker: Priya Nair
sessions.description: Moving beyond RAG into context engineering. Covers memory integration, tool result handling, context assembly patterns, and continuous agent improvement.

sessions.title: Enterprise Knowledge Graphs for AI Agents
sessions.speaker: Dr. Akiko Tanaka
sessions.description: How Knowledge Graphs complement RAG systems for entity resolution, state tracking, and decision support. Preview of Fabric ontology integration.

sessions.title: Multimodal RAG: Chat with Your PowerPoints
sessions.speaker: Carlos Rivera
sessions.description: Indexing and retrieving from images, charts, tables, and presentations. Covers content verbalization, image understanding, and hybrid retrieval.

sessions.title: MCP and Federated Knowledge Sources
sessions.speaker: Sarah Kim
sessions.description: Using Model Context Protocol (MCP) to federate across SharePoint, Bing, custom APIs, and third-party data. Live demo of multi-source agent grounding.

sessions.title: Evaluating RAG Quality at Scale
sessions.speaker: Dr. Thomas Weber
sessions.description: Automated evaluation frameworks for retrieval quality, answer faithfulness, and citation accuracy. Practical guide to building RAG eval pipelines.

sessions.title: Work IQ: Connecting M365 to Your Agents
sessions.speaker: Rebecca Strauss
sessions.description: Federation to SharePoint, email, Teams messages, and the full M365 Graph. How Work IQ extends Foundry agents with organizational knowledge.

sessions.title: Memory Systems for Agents: From V1 to V2
sessions.speaker: Lewis Liu
sessions.description: Evolution of agent memory. Thread storage, episodic memory, execution-focused memory, and continuous self-improvement patterns.

sessions.title: Closing Keynote: The Future of Knowledge Retrieval
sessions.speaker: Pablo Castro
sessions.description: From search() to ground(). The vision for Knowledge Retrieval as the foundation of enterprise AI agents. What's next for Foundry IQ.

sessions.title: Fabric IQ: Unifying Structured and Unstructured Data
sessions.speaker: Lisa Park
sessions.description: How Fabric IQ bridges the gap between your lakehouse data and AI agents. NL2SQL, semantic layers, and OneLake catalog discovery.

sessions.title: NL2SQL for AI Agents: Best Practices
sessions.speaker: Ahmed Hassan
sessions.description: Patterns for reliable natural language to SQL conversion. Covers schema understanding, query validation, and error recovery for agent scenarios.

sessions.title: Real-Time Data Pipelines for Agent Grounding
sessions.speaker: Nina Petrov
sessions.description: Keeping your knowledge bases fresh. Change tracking, incremental indexing, and streaming updates for enterprise data sources.

sessions.title: Multi-Cloud Data Access with OneLake
sessions.speaker: Raj Krishnan
sessions.description: Accessing AWS S3, GCP, and Snowflake data through OneLake shortcuts. Unified governance and access control for cross-cloud agents.

sessions.title: Data Governance for AI Agents
sessions.speaker: Michelle Torres
sessions.description: Document-level ACLs, Purview sensitivity labels, and compliance frameworks for enterprise agent deployments.

sessions.title: Vector Compression and Billion-Scale Indexes
sessions.speaker: Dr. Wei Zhang
sessions.description: Quantization, Matryoshka representation learning, and filtered vector search at scale. How Azure AI Search handles billion-vector workloads.

sessions.title: Custom Skills and Content Enrichment Pipelines
sessions.speaker: Olivia Durand
sessions.description: Building custom skillsets for Azure AI Search. Entity extraction, classification, summarization, and custom enrichment with Azure AI Services.

sessions.title: Zero Trust for AI Agents
sessions.speaker: David Okafor
sessions.description: Applying Zero Trust principles to agent architectures. Network isolation, managed identity, and private endpoint patterns for Foundry.

sessions.title: Document-Level Access Control in RAG
sessions.speaker: Elena Volkova
sessions.description: Implementing per-document security trimming in Azure AI Search. ACL propagation from SharePoint, Blob Storage, and custom sources.

sessions.title: Red Teaming Your AI Agents
sessions.speaker: Marcus Wright
sessions.description: Practical guide to adversarial testing of agents. Prompt injection, tool misuse, data exfiltration, and how to build guardrails.

sessions.title: Compliance Frameworks for Enterprise AI
sessions.speaker: Patricia Mendez
sessions.description: HIPAA, SOC2, GDPR, and FedRAMP considerations for agent deployments. Certification requirements and compliance architecture patterns.

sessions.title: Securing MCP Connections and Tool Calls
sessions.speaker: Anika Gupta
sessions.description: Authentication patterns for MCP servers, OAuth flows for tool authorization, and securing the agent-to-tool communication channel.

sessions.title: Serverless AI Search: Zero to Scale
sessions.speaker: Anna Bergström
sessions.description: Preview of serverless Azure AI Search. Scale-to-zero, usage-based pricing, and auto-provisioning for Foundry agents.

sessions.title: Private Agent Deployments on Azure
sessions.speaker: Tomás Gutierrez
sessions.description: End-to-end private networking for Foundry agents. VNet integration, private endpoints, and Bastion-based testing.

sessions.title: Cost Optimization for RAG Workloads
sessions.speaker: Yuki Yamamoto
sessions.description: Right-sizing search services, vector compression techniques, and consumption monitoring for production RAG systems.

sessions.title: Monitoring and Observability for Agents
sessions.speaker: Ji-Hoon Park
sessions.description: Tracing agent runs, monitoring retrieval quality, alerting on regressions, and building operational dashboards for production agents.

sessions.title: Disaster Recovery for Agent Systems
sessions.speaker: Michael Okonkwo
sessions.description: High availability patterns, geo-redundancy, and failover strategies for production agent systems using Foundry and Azure AI Search.

sessions.title: Agent Framework Deep Dive: Python SDK
sessions.speaker: Fatima Al-Rashid
sessions.description: Complete walkthrough of the Microsoft Agent Framework Python SDK. Agent creation, tool registration, memory, and deployment patterns.

sessions.title: GitHub Copilot + Foundry IQ: Code with Context
sessions.speaker: Amanda Liu
sessions.description: Using Foundry IQ Knowledge Bases as context in GitHub Copilot CLI. Domain-aware code generation, test creation, and documentation grounded in your enterprise data.

sessions.title: CI/CD for AI Agents
sessions.speaker: Kevin O'Brien
sessions.description: Automated testing, evaluation gates, and deployment pipelines for agent applications. Integration with GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps.

sessions.title: MCP Server Development Workshop
sessions.speaker: Chris Patel
sessions.description: Hands-on workshop: build your own MCP server to expose a custom data source to Foundry agents. Covers protocol, auth, and testing.

sessions.title: Building SaaS on Foundry: ISV Patterns
sessions.speaker: Marco Rossi
sessions.description: Multi-tenant agent architectures, per-customer knowledge bases, usage-based billing, and scaling patterns for ISVs building on Foundry.

sessions.title: From Prototype to Production: Agent Deployment Guide
sessions.speaker: Sophie Chen
sessions.description: Complete guide to taking your agent from playground prototype to production deployment. Covers evaluation, guardrails, monitoring, and scaling.
